quote: I am the way, the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father, except through me.
why: Jesus makes this claim to Thomas at the Last Supper, in answer to the question of how to know the way to the Father.

quote: I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in me will still live, even if he dies.
why: Jesus says this to Martha outside Bethany, just before he raises her brother Lazarus from the dead.

quote: My soul magnifies the Lord.
why: Mary sings this opening line of the Magnificat while visiting her relative Elizabeth.

quote: Behold, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!
why: John the Baptist says this on seeing Jesus approach him at the Jordan.

quote: Lord, to whom would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
why: Peter answers this way after many disciples desert Jesus over his hard teaching about eating his flesh.

quote: Repent and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
why: Peter calls the Pentecost crowd to repentance after they ask what they should do.

quote: There is salvation in no one else, for there is no other name under heaven that is given among men, by which we must be saved!
why: Peter declares this to the Sanhedrin after healing a lame beggar in Jesus’ name.

quote: It is written, ‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceeds out of God’s mouth.’
why: Jesus answers the tempter in the wilderness by quoting Deuteronomy.

quote: Father, forgive them, for they don’t know what they are doing.
why: Jesus prays this from the cross as the soldiers divide his clothing.

quote: It is finished!
why: These are Jesus’ last words before he bows his head and dies on the cross.

quote: My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?
why: Jesus cries this out in Aramaic from the cross, quoting the opening of Psalm 22.

quote: You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.
why: Simon Peter answers Jesus’ question “Who do you say that I am?” at Caesarea Philippi.

quote: Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me, for you are not setting your mind on the things of God, but on the things of men.
why: Jesus rebukes Peter for objecting to his prediction of suffering and death.

quote: Behold, the servant of the Lord; let it be done to me according to your word.
why: Mary responds this way to the angel Gabriel’s announcement that she will bear the Messiah.

quote: Glory to God in the highest, on earth peace, good will toward men.
why: A multitude of the heavenly host praise God after the announcement of Jesus’ birth to the shepherds.

quote: My Lord and my God!
why: Thomas responds this way once he touches the risen Jesus’ wounds, having doubted the other disciples’ report.

quote: Saul, Saul, why do you persecute me?
why: A voice from heaven says this to Saul on the road to Damascus; Jesus identifies himself in the next verse.

quote: Who are you, Lord?
why: Saul asks this after being knocked to the ground by a light from heaven on the road to Damascus.

quote: Lord Jesus, receive my spirit!
why: Stephen prays this as he is being stoned to death, the first Christian martyr.

quote: Believe in the Lord Jesus Christ, and you will be saved, you and your household.
why: Paul and Silas answer the Philippian jailer’s question after an earthquake opens the prison doors.

quote: This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased.
why: A voice from heaven says this immediately after Jesus is baptized by John in the Jordan.

quote: Cheer up! It is I! Don’t be afraid.
why: Jesus calls this out to his terrified disciples as he walks toward their boat on the water.

quote: Peace! Be still!
why: Jesus speaks this to the wind and waves during a storm on the Sea of Galilee, and they obey.

quote: My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass away from me; nevertheless, not what I desire, but what you desire.
why: Jesus prays this in Gethsemane the night before his crucifixion.

quote: Take, eat; this is my body.
why: Jesus says this while instituting the Lord’s Supper with his disciples.

quote: Lord, you know everything. You know that I have affection for you.
why: Peter answers this way, grieved, the third time the risen Jesus asks if he loves him.

quote: I am innocent of the blood of this righteous person. You see to it.
why: Pilate says this while washing his hands before the crowd at Jesus’ trial.

quote: Let him be crucified!
why: The crowd shouts this back when Pilate asks what he should do with Jesus.

quote: What is truth?
why: Pilate asks this rhetorically after Jesus tells him he came to testify to the truth.

quote: Lord, remember me when you come into your Kingdom.
choices: one of the criminals crucified with Jesus
why: One of the two criminals crucified beside Jesus asks to be remembered in his Kingdom.

quote: Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, Lord.
why: Peter falls at Jesus’ knees after a miraculous catch of fish overwhelms the boats.

quote: Let there be light
why: This is God’s first recorded command in creation, on the first day.

quote: The serpent deceived me, and I ate.
why: Eve answers Yahweh this way after she and Adam eat the forbidden fruit.

quote: Am I my brother’s keeper?
why: Cain answers this way when Yahweh asks where his brother is, after murdering him.

quote: Here is the fire and the wood, but where is the lamb for a burnt offering?
why: Isaac asks his father this on the way up the mountain, carrying the wood for the offering.

quote: God will provide himself the lamb for a burnt offering, my son.
why: Abraham answers Isaac’s question about the sacrifice on the way up Mount Moriah.

quote: I AM WHO I AM
why: God gives this name for himself to Moses at the burning bush on Mount Horeb.

quote: ‘Let my people go, that they may hold a feast to me in the wilderness.’
why: Moses and Aaron deliver this demand to Pharaoh on Yahweh’s behalf.

quote: Haven’t I commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Don’t be afraid. Don’t be dismayed, for Yahweh your God is with you wherever you go.
why: Yahweh commissions Joshua this way as he takes over leadership of Israel after Moses’ death.

quote: If it seems evil to you to serve Yahweh, choose today whom you will serve; whether the gods which your fathers served that were beyond the River, or the gods of the Amorites, in whose land you dwell; but as for me and my house, we will serve Yahweh.
why: Joshua declares this to the tribes of Israel gathered at Shechem near the end of his life.

quote: If I am shaved, then my strength will go from me and I will become weak, and be like any other man.
why: Samson finally reveals the secret of his strength to Delilah, who then betrays him to the Philistines.

quote: for where you go, I will go; and where you stay, I will stay. Your people will be my people, and your God my God.
why: Ruth pledges her loyalty to Naomi rather than return to Moab.

quote: Speak; for your servant hears.
why: The boy Samuel answers this way once he recognizes it is Yahweh calling him in the night.

quote: You come to me with a sword, with a spear, and with a javelin; but I come to you in the name of Yahweh of Armies, the God of the armies of Israel, whom you have defied.
why: David answers Goliath’s taunts before the fight in the Valley of Elah.

quote: Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to listen than the fat of rams.
why: Samuel rebukes Saul for disobeying Yahweh’s command regarding the Amalekites.

quote: You are the man! This is what Yahweh, the God of Israel, says: ‘I anointed you king over Israel, and I delivered you out of the hand of Saul.
why: The prophet Nathan confronts King David after telling him a parable about a rich man and a poor man’s lamb.

quote: Give your servant therefore an understanding heart to judge your people, that I may discern between good and evil; for who is able to judge this great people of yours?
why: Solomon asks God for this at Gibeon, when Yahweh offers to give him whatever he asks.

quote: How long will you waver between the two sides? If Yahweh is God, follow him; but if Baal, then follow him.
why: Elijah challenges the people of Israel on Mount Carmel to choose between Yahweh and Baal.

quote: I have been very jealous for Yahweh, the God of Armies; for the children of Israel have forsaken your covenant, thrown down your altars, and killed your prophets with the sword. I, even I only, am left; and they seek my life, to take it away.
why: Elijah says this to Yahweh in a cave on Mount Horeb after fleeing from Jezebel.

quote: Please let a double portion of your spirit be on me.
why: Elisha makes this request of Elijah just before his mentor is taken up to heaven.

quote: Naked I came out of my mother’s womb, and naked will I return there. Yahweh gave, and Yahweh has taken away. Blessed be Yahweh’s name.
why: Job says this after losing his children and all his possessions in a single day.

quote: Here I am. Send me!
why: Isaiah volunteers this way after seeing a vision of Yahweh’s throne and having his lips cleansed.

quote: Woe is me! For I am undone, because I am a man of unclean lips and I live among a people of unclean lips, for my eyes have seen the King, Yahweh of Armies!
why: Isaiah cries this out on seeing a vision of Yahweh seated on his throne in the temple.

quote: Ah, Lord Yahweh! Behold, I don’t know how to speak; for I am a child.
why: Jeremiah protests this way when Yahweh first calls him to be a prophet.

quote: If it happens, our God whom we serve is able to deliver us from the burning fiery furnace; and he will deliver us out of your hand, O king.
why: The three men say this to Nebuchadnezzar before being thrown into the fiery furnace for refusing to worship his golden image.

quote: My God has sent his angel, and has shut the lions’ mouths, and they have not hurt me, because innocence was found in me before him; and also before you, O king, I have done no harm.
why: Daniel says this to King Darius after surviving a night in the lions’ den.

quote: I am a Hebrew, and I fear Yahweh, the God of heaven, who has made the sea and the dry land.
why: Jonah identifies himself this way to the terrified sailors after the storm arises.

quote: Therefore now, Yahweh, take, I beg you, my life from me, for it is better for me to die than to live.
why: Jonah asks to die, angry that Yahweh spared Nineveh after Jonah’s warning of judgment.

quote: if I perish, I perish.
why: Esther says this to Mordecai as she resolves to approach the king uninvited to plead for her people.

quote: I am Joseph, your brother, whom you sold into Egypt.
why: Joseph finally reveals his identity to the brothers who sold him into slavery in Egypt years earlier.

quote: I am Esau your firstborn. I have done what you asked me to do. Please arise, sit and eat of my venison, that your soul may bless me.
why: Jacob says this to his blind father Isaac while impersonating his brother Esau to steal the blessing.

quote: Yahweh of Armies, if you will indeed look at the affliction of your servant and remember me, and not forget your servant, but will give to your servant a boy, then I will give him to Yahweh all the days of his life, and no razor shall come on his head.
why: Hannah makes this vow at the tabernacle in Shiloh, weeping over her childlessness.

quote: Should a man like me flee? Who is there that, being such as I, would go into the temple to save his life? I will not go in.
why: Nehemiah refuses to hide in the temple when enemies plot to intimidate him during the wall’s rebuilding.

quote: What have I done to you, that you have struck me these three times?
why: Yahweh opens the mouth of Balaam’s donkey to question him after he strikes it three times.

quote: O Lord, how shall I save Israel? Behold, my family is the poorest in Manasseh, and I am the least in my father’s house.
why: Gideon protests his own weakness after the angel of Yahweh calls him a mighty man of valor.
